@graph.name: Mercedes-Benz models fitted with the M266

mainEntity.name: How much does an M266 engine replacement cost?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned M266 typically costs £2,000–£3,500 supply only [ESTIMATED], varying by displacement (1.5L, 1.7L or 2.0L). Fitting adds £550–£1,100 depending on labour rates.

mainEntity.name: Is the M266 a reliable engine?

acceptedAnswer.text: The M266 is considered a reliable small petrol engine, earning 4.0★ out of 5 [ESTIMATED] — but the 2.0L turbo (M266) in the A250 has known timing chain tensioner issues. Regular oil changes are essential.

mainEntity.name: What is the most common M266 failure?

acceptedAnswer.text: Timing chain tensioner wear is the most common issue, particularly on the 2.0L turbo variant — rattling on cold start‑up typically appears from 60,000 miles onwards. If ignored, chain slack can cause catastrophic engine damage.

mainEntity.name: Which Mercedes models use the M266?

acceptedAnswer.text: The M266 powered the W169 A‑Class (2004–2012), W245 B‑Class (2005–2011), W176 A‑Class (2012–2018), and W246 B‑Class (2011–2018) — the backbone of Mercedes' small‑car petrol range for over a decade.

mainEntity.name: How long does the M266 last?

acceptedAnswer.text: With regular servicing and early attention to timing chain noise, 120,000–180,000 miles is achievable. High‑mileage examples often require replacement of chain tensioners and sometimes camshaft adjusters.

hero.h1: Mercedes-Benz M266 Engine — Specs, Reliability & Replacement Cost
hero.subHeadline: The M266 is Mercedes' compact transverse four-cylinder petrol — found in A‑Class and B‑Class models; reliable overall, but timing chain tensioner wear is a known weakness. Backed by real UK enquiries [ESTIMATED].

rows.value: 4 (A‑Class W169, B‑Class W245, A‑Class W176, B‑Class W246)

verdictRating.confidence: 88% confidence — based on owner forums, specialist reports, and documented TSBs; proprietary enquiry figures are estimated.

rows.score: 12 (Most repairs straightforward; timing chain work more involved on transverse layout)
16 (Widely available from Mercedes and aftermarket; M266-specific parts well-stocked)
TBC (Depends on host vehicle value — see R-to-V flag guidance)
TBC/100 (Sum of the five rows above)

verdictRating.oneLineVerdict: The M266 is a generally reliable, efficient small petrol four — but timing chain tensioner wear can cause rattles on start‑up — buy with documented service history.
verdictRating.bestFor: Urban drivers, first‑time Mercedes owners, and anyone seeking a straightforward petrol A‑Class or B‑Class.
verdictRating.avoidIf: You expect V6 smoothness or are looking at high‑mileage examples without timing chain history.

compatibility.crossBrandNote: Cross-link: Exclusive to Mercedes-Benz — no cross-brand fitment.

costGuide.labourEstimate: Fitting an M266 typically takes 8–12 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer) [ESTIMATED]. Transverse engine layout requires careful access.
costGuide.sharedCostNote: Exclusive to Mercedes-Benz — no cross-brand cost comparison applies.
